title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: In the arid savannas of Africa, termites build structures that (0) defy conventional engineering logic. These towering mounds, which can reach several meters in height, are (1) to an intricate system of ventilation shafts. For years, scientists were (2) as to how these tiny insects could maintain a constant internal temperature. Recent studies have (3) that the mounds act as a giant lung, inhaling and exhaling air as the external temperature fluctuates. This natural air-conditioning system is so effective that it has (4) the attention of modern architects looking for sustainable building solutions. By (5) the principles of termite architecture, designers can reduce the energy consumption of large buildings in hot climates. This approach, known as biomimicry, (6) on the idea that nature has already solved many of our most complex technical problems. The mounds are not just functional; they are also incredibly (7) and can withstand harsh weather conditions for decades. Studying these insects (8) us with valuable lessons about efficiency and cooperation. It is a reminder that even the smallest creatures can produce extraordinary feats of design, proving that we have much to learn from the natural world if we take the time to observe the hidden complexity within the most seemingly simple biological structures.

example.explanation: “Defy logic” is a fixed collocation meaning something is impossible to explain.

questions.explanation: “Be home to” is a fixed expression meaning that a place contains something.
“Puzzled as to how” is a common academic structure for expressing confusion.
“Confirmed” is the correct verb for verifying a scientific hypothesis.
“Caught the attention” is the standard idiom for attracting interest.
“Applying principles” is the correct collocation for using rules in a new context.
“Draws on [an idea]” means to use something as a source or basis for work.
“Resilient” is the best adjective for a structure that survives harsh conditions.
“Provides us with” is the correct grammatical structure for giving information.
